Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jan-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Donovan Murray, Sr. and Anne Murray
Annie C. Wu of DEHAY & ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / opposing party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
(Refer to Sign-in Sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per CCP 36( a ) and ( d ). Jury Trial is April 9, 2014, Wednesday, at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day of trial is May 9, 2014.

Last day for hearing summary motions is April 3, 2014. Plaintiffs stipulated that Summary motions may be brought on 20 days notice without additional two days for e-service. E-Service is deemed equivalent of hand-service.

Time to respond to written contention discovery is shortened to 20 days, only for newly served discovery as of today.

Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than Feb 28, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 7, 2014. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on April 3, 2014.

Fact discovery cut-off date is March 26, 2014. Expert discovery cut off remains open until the day of trial, by 5:00 p.m. Pacific Time.

No later than January 21, 2014, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than January 21, 2014, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown.

Before any motions in limine are filed with the court the defendants are ordered to meet and confer on all motions in limine in order to avoid frivolous and duplicative hearings. If the parties do not meet and confer on the motions in limine the court will take appropriate actions. Each side will be limited to a total of 5 motions, not to exceed 5 pages each motion. All motions are due on date of trial assignment. If a defendant has a case specific MIL, the defendant will need to take it up with the trial judge for determination on the day of trial assignment. For Page & Line Designations, parties are to meet and confer on the day of trial assignment. Then in 3 days parties to present to the trial judge in writing of the agreed designations and anything in dispute.

Further case management conference on February 19, 2014 at 1:30pm in Dept. 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. Counsel for plaintiffs to notify the Court by 2:00 p.m. on Feb. 18 by phone to cancel the conference if there is no discovery issue to discuss.

There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on April 9, 2014 at 1:30pm in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by April 7, 2014 by 4:00pm. Counsel must contact Settlement Manager Pang Ly to obtain Court's approval if a client needs to attend the conference by phone. Plaintiffs to prepare and forward a proposed order to Counsel Annie Wu (Dehay Elliston), for approval as to form. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
